compiled_state.description:
# Publish the review policy: what the LLM-Review and Verified gates mean for contributors

## Why

rebar's blocking `LLM-Review` Gerrit label appears to be first-of-kind in public OSS: research (2024–2026) found no project documenting a blocking LLM merge vote — the Kubernetes AI-review policy (https://github.com/kubernetes/community/blob/master/github-management/ai-code-review-tools.md) makes AI review advisory-only. Novelty raises the documentation bar. The document's SHAPE is convergent: Chromium's CQ doc (https://chromium.googlesource.com/chromium/src/+/main/docs/infra/cq.md) and Zuul's gating doc (https://zuul-ci.org/docs/zuul/latest/gating.html) both name the labels, define each vote value, state bot-vs-human authority, and document escape hatches.

Why a separate doc rather than expanding CONTRIBUTING §2c: §2c is the in-flow quick reference; the policy doc carries the normative content (authority, dispute, bypass, responsibility) that outlives workflow edits and is linkable from every surface. Authority rule (the epic's single-source protocol, clarified): `docs/review-policy.md` is the sole authoritative DOCUMENT for vote semantics; its table's CONTENT is transcribed from the code (the enums below) — document-of-record and source-of-truth are different things and both are named here.

Sequencing note (resolves the file-existence review findings): `docs/your-first-change.md` does not exist at plan time — it is created by story **fat-leaflike-canary**, which this story `depends_on` (edge recorded in the store). The retarget edit to that file executes only after that story lands.

## What

New `docs/review-policy.md` (contributor-facing):

- The two required labels, described accurately:
  - `Verified` — CI on GitHub Actions via `gerrit-verify.yaml`. NOT described as just three make targets: the workflow additionally runs the rebar-ticket trailer check, module-size gate, prompt-index/env-registry drift gates, security-rules freshness, criteria-routing parity, public-types drift, `make config-check`, pip-audit, and unit + integration pytest tiers. The doc states: "locally approximate with `make lint && make typecheck && make test`; the authoritative list is the workflow file (linked)."
  - `LLM-Review` — what the bot reviews; only bots/admins vote either label (no self-approval, no self-verification).
- Vote-semantics table (Chromium style) transcribed from BOTH code sources: the five `_TAG_SUFFIXES` coverage-gap sub-reasons in `src/rebar/review_bot/adapter.py` (gate-disabled, llm-unavailable, scanner, review-error, indeterminate), PLUS the merge-path tags emitted by `src/rebar/review_bot/voter.py`: the distinct `coverage-gap (merge-review)` veto (~line 160) and the `(merge-change, N integrated commit(s))` suffix variant. Each row: what it means + the productive response (`finding` → fix and re-push; any `coverage-gap (…)` → infra veto, not your code, maintainer re-triggers; `Verified −1` → open the linked run; `recheck` for flakes). Drift-guard line in the doc: "if you see a tag not listed here, treat it as coverage-gap-class and ping the maintainer" (a CI drift gate tying doc to enums is out of scope, noted as future work).
- Bot vs human authority (Zuul's mechanical-enforcement framing): (a) the lead maintainer sets the rubric and can waive/override any finding; (b) dispute path — resolve the comment thread with written justification or raise to the maintainer, best-effort response within 5 business days (matching SECURITY.md's ack window); if unresolved, the maintainer's decision is final (solo-maintainer reality, stated plainly); (c) the gate supplements maintainer judgment; (d) bypasses are admin-only and auditable.
- Responsibility clause (Kubernetes norm, cited): the submitter must be able to personally explain their change, AI-assisted or not.
- One clarifying paragraph distinguishing THIS (code-review merge gate) from the plan-review ticket gate, linking docs/plan-review-gate.md.
- Framing recorded in the doc itself: the gate is "required-with-human-override"; the dispute path is v1 and will iterate with the first external contributor.

Links this story owns (ownership-inversion rule): add the review-policy link into CONTRIBUTING.md §2c (slimmed to summary + pointer; the full sub-reason enumeration moves out of §2c) and — after the dependency lands — retarget `docs/your-first-change.md`'s vote-semantics link from §2c to the new doc.

## Scope

IN: `docs/review-policy.md` (new), CONTRIBUTING §2c slim-down + pointer, tutorial link retarget (executes post fat-leaflike-canary).
OUT: changing gate behavior/thresholds/criteria; a CI drift gate tying the doc to the enums (future work); the plan-review gate's own docs; Gerrit ACL changes.

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] `docs/review-policy.md` present — prove, each as its own grep against the file: "LLM-Review"; "Verified"; "gate-disabled"; "llm-unavailable"; "scanner"; "review-error"; "indeterminate"; "merge-review"; "merge-change"; "recheck"; "5 business days"; "plan-review"; "override"; "dispute"; "bypass".
- [ ] Verified described via the workflow — prove: `grep -q "gerrit-verify" docs/review-policy.md` and `grep -qi "approximat" docs/review-policy.md` (the make targets labeled as local approximation).
- [ ] No-self-approval + maintainer-override stated — prove: `grep -qi "self-approv" docs/review-policy.md` and `grep -qi "waive\|override" docs/review-policy.md`.
- [ ] Single-source enforced — prove: `grep -q "review-policy" CONTRIBUTING.md` (pointer added) and `grep -c "llm-unavailable\|gate-disabled" CONTRIBUTING.md` = 0 after the slim-down (enumeration lives only in the policy doc); post-dependency, `grep -q "review-policy" docs/your-first-change.md`.
- [ ] Framing + iteration caveat in the doc — prove: `grep -qi "human-override" docs/review-policy.md` and `grep -qi "will iterate\|v1 of this policy" docs/review-policy.md`.
compiled_state.title: Publish the review policy: what the LLM-Review and Verified gates mean for contributors
